title: Proposal: Add this repository to Podman Container Tools CNCF Project
body:
Per the Podman Container Tools governance (located [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md)) projects that are strongly related to Podman, appropriately licensed, high quality, and under active development are eligible to be added to the Podman Container Tools project. The container-libs project meets all these criteria. It is also a critical part of the infrastructure of Podman, Buildah, and Skopeo, containing the libraries that allow all 3 of these to function.

As a Core Maintainer of Podman and a maintainer of the container-tools repository, I recommend that this repository be added to the Podman Container Tools project. Maintainers of container-libs can vote for or against in this issue.

comments.body: Voting can conclude Friday morning and, if accepted, we can do a Core Maintainers vote Friday.

Same question here, should this propose a the initial set of maintainers here so we know who we voting for?

cc @containers/core-maintainers
As with the other repo: I would propose that the list of people I tagged are Maintainers of the repo. Logic behind selection: Contributors from the last year with multiple significant PRs merged.

+1 to adding the project.

The proposed maintainer list also looks reasonable — and per that criterion, @mheon should also be included.

Well the core maintainers will have full maintainer access no matter what and are not listed again as maintainer in the Maintainers file as they will shown as core maintainers.

As for the people pinged that are not core maintainers I am in favor to add them as maintainers and to add the project to the CNCF org.
+1 to adding this project, and I mirror @Luap99 's thinking on the maintainers list that this starts with.

Not for this but maybe a long term process question: I wonder if we need to repeat core maintainers and community managers on every repo? This will require a lot of repo updates if we add/remove people from these rules.
Done via #883 - on Monday I will add this to the Podman governance as a subproject to complete the project.
